name: Mockery
description: Flexible PHP mock-object framework with Hamcrest-style matchers, supporting partial mocks, static-method mocks and chained expectations.

seo.title: Mockery — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Mockery: Flexible PHP mock-object framework with Hamcrest-style matchers, supporting partial mocks, static-method mocks and chained expectations.

when_to_use: PHP mock objects with fluent expectation DSL
Partial mocks for legacy PHP class isolation
Static method mocking with overload mocks
Hamcrest-style matchers for flexible argument assertions

pros: Handles partial and static mocks well
cons: More flexible means easier to misuse
Static mocks couple tests to globals
Overlap with PHPUnit's own mocks confuses newcomers
Debugging failed expectations can be cryptic
review.verdict: The go-to PHP mocking lib when PHPUnit mocks run out of steam, used with the usual mock-overuse caveat.
review.best_for: PHP teams needing expressive mocks for legacy or static-heavy code
review.not_for: Projects where PHPUnit's built-in mocking is already sufficient
